name: How to Bake a Perfect Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 (8 oz) packages Philadelphia brand cream cheese
Cornstarch (1 tablespoon per 2 packages of cream cheese)
Large eggs (as specified in the recipe or standard for a cheesecake)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wrap the pan with foil to keep it watertight.
2. Grease the sides of the pan first so the surface does not crack when the cake starts to shrink during cooling. It always shrinks slightly.
3. Chill thoroughly before unmolding. For best results, make the cheesecake one day ahead and leave it in the springform pan until ready to use.
4. Prepare a water bath: Place the baking dish with no more than 1 inch of water into the oven. Ensure the water bath pan is not higher than the springform pan; otherwise, it may slow down baking.
5. Verify your oven temperature accuracy.
6. When the cheesecake is done, turn off the oven and do not open the door. Let it sit for 1 hour to cool gradually. Then remove, place on a rack, and let cool to room temperature (about 1 hour). Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate overnight.
This recipe ensures a perfect, non-falling cheesecake when all tips are followed correctly.
